description: Common-sense reference that applies to most modern condensing combi and system boilers. Use this when the exact brand or model isn't known.

faultCodes.title: Boiler locked out (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Boiler's shut down and needs a reset. If it locks out again, stop resetting and find the fault.
likelyCauses: Won't light — no gas, bad electrode, or blocked condensate
Overheating — poor flow or sludge in the system
Pressure too low — leak or knackered expansion vessel
Fan or flue problem — blockage or worn bearings
PCB flagging a dodgy sensor, valve, or wiring
quickChecks: Read the fault code on screen — check the brand-specific page for that code
Check gas, electrics, and water pressure before you reset
Reset once. If it locks out again within 10 mins, don't keep trying — diagnose it
Check the condensate pipe isn't frozen or blocked (classic winter job)

faultCodes.title: Ignition fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Boiler won't light or can't hold a flame. Most common fault across all brands.
likelyCauses: No gas or low gas pressure — try the hob or other appliances
Ignition electrode worn, cracked, or dirty
Flame sensor dirty or glazed — can't see the flame
Condensate trap blocked — water backing up into the burner
Gas valve stuck or coil faulty
Frozen condensate pipe — happens all winter
quickChecks: Try another gas appliance. No gas anywhere? Call the supplier
Clean and re-gap the ignition and flame sensor electrode(s)
Pull out and clean the condensate trap and siphon
Check inlet gas pressure at the test point on load — should be 20 mbar (nat gas)
Cold weather? Check the outside condensate pipe for ice — pour warm water to thaw

faultCodes.title: Overheat / limit trip (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Flow temp went above the limit (usually 95–105°C). Almost always a circulation problem.
likelyCauses: Pump failed, airlocked, or a valve shut somewhere
Heat exchanger clogged with scale or magnetite
Overheat stat or flow NTC giving false readings
Bypass set too high — not enough flow through rads
quickChecks: Feel the pump — should be warm and vibrating when it's running
Check system pressure is 1.0–1.5 bar cold
Bleed rads and the auto air vent — trapped air kills circulation
Bleed a rad — black water means magnetite. System needs a flush
Feel flow and return pipes — big temp gap (>20°C) means poor flow

faultCodes.title: Low pressure (generic)
faultCodes.summary: System pressure dropped below 0.5 bar. Boiler won't fire until you top it up.
likelyCauses: Slow leak — rad valve, pipe joint, or auto air vent dripping
Expansion vessel gone — diaphragm failed or Schrader valve leaking
PRV letting by — check the discharge pipe outside
Bled rads recently without topping up
quickChecks: Top up to 1.0–1.5 bar via the filling loop. Close it when done!
Walk the system — check every joint and valve for damp spots
Look at the PRV discharge pipe outside. Dripping? PRV needs replacing
Check expansion vessel charge — tyre gauge on the Schrader valve, should be ~0.75 bar cold
Keeps dropping every week? You need a proper leak search

faultCodes.title: Fan fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Fan isn't hitting the right speed. Boiler won't try to light without correct airflow.
likelyCauses: Worn fan bearings — common after 8–10 years
Blocked flue — debris, bird's nest, or ice
Tacho or wiring fault — loose plug or damaged cable
PCB fan circuit failed
Condensate pooling in the fan housing
quickChecks: Listen on start-up — grinding or scraping means worn bearings
Power off, spin the fan by hand. Should turn freely with no drag
Check flue terminal outside for blockages or condensate build-up
Check 230V to the fan and tacho signal back to PCB
Look at the fan connector — corrosion on tacho pins is common

faultCodes.title: Sensor fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Sensor's reading out of range. Boiler might limp along or lock out.
likelyCauses: NTC gone — open circuit reads high, short reads low
Plug come loose — vibration shakes them free
Wiring damaged — often after other work near the boiler
Moisture in the connector — causes corrosion and jumpy readings
quickChecks: Push the sensor connector back in firmly — should click
Meter the NTC — roughly 10kΩ at 25°C (check the maker's chart)
Inspect wiring for damage, especially near hot surfaces
Green corrosion on pins? Clean with contact cleaner or swap it
Sensor reads fine? Suspect the PCB socket

faultCodes.title: Condensate blockage (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Condensate can't drain, so water backs up into the boiler. Number one winter call-out.
likelyCauses: Frozen condensate pipe outside — classic winter fault
Condensate trap blocked with muck or sludge
Pipe run too long, no fall, or undersized
Internal siphon blocked
quickChecks: Pour warm (not boiling) water over the outside condensate pipe to thaw it
Pull out and clean the condensate trap inside the boiler
Check the pipe has a steady fall — no dips where water can sit and freeze
Keeps happening? Recommend 32mm pipe or trace heating
Emergency fix: run the condensate into a bucket and tell the customer

faultCodes.title: Pump / circulation fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Water isn't moving through the system. Pump's failed, airlocked, or something's blocking flow.
likelyCauses: Pump seized — very common after sitting idle all summer
Airlock in pump housing — impeller can't spin
System filter packed with magnetite sludge
Zone valve or lockshield shut — blocking flow
quickChecks: Feel the pump — should be warm and vibrating when running
Bleed the pump via the screw on the front (small flathead)
Give it a gentle tap — a stuck impeller sometimes frees up
Check the system filter if there is one — clean out any sludge
Make sure all zone valves are open and lockshields aren't shut
